record.field: EspoCRM is a lightweight, self-hosted CRM for managing accounts, contacts, leads, opportunities, activities and email from one clean web interface.

EspoCRM is a lightweight, self-hosted CRM for managing accounts, contacts, leads, opportunities, activities and email from one clean web interface.

EspoCRM is an open-source customer relationship management platform for small and mid-sized businesses that want to track their sales pipeline, organise customer data and coordinate commercial processes without the weight of larger suites. Built on PHP with a fast single-page interface, it covers the full sales cycle — from leads and opportunities to accounts, contacts, calls, meetings and after-sales support — while keeping every record inside your own database.

Key features
  • Sales automation: leads, opportunities, accounts, contacts and a visual pipeline
  • Activities and calendar: calls, meetings, tasks and shared team scheduling
  • Built-in email client with two-way IMAP/SMTP integration and mass email campaigns
  • Workflows, business process automation (BPM) and custom entities via the layout manager
  • Role-based access control, teams and field-level permissions for sensitive data
  • REST API, import/export, reports, dashboards and a background daemon for scheduled jobs
Best for

Small and mid-sized teams that need a focused, easy-to-run CRM they fully control. Because the CRM holds personal and commercial data, keep it behind HTTPS and configure SMTP and the admin password on first login; the bundled daemon container runs EspoCRM's scheduled jobs and must stay up.

services: db: image: mysql:8.0 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' environment: MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: {{ mysql_root_password }} MYSQL_DATABASE: {{ db_name }} MYSQL_USER: {{ db_user }} MYSQL_PASSWORD: {{ db_password }} volumes: - ./db-data:/var/lib/mysql expose: - 3306 healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "mysqladmin", "ping", "-h", "localhost"] interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_period: 30s restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal espocrm: image: espocrm/espocrm:latest container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy environment: ESPOCRM_DATABASE_PLATFORM: Mysql ESPOCRM_DATABASE_HOST: db ESPOCRM_DATABASE_NAME: {{ db_name }} ESPOCRM_DATABASE_USER: {{ db_user }} ESPOCRM_DATABASE_PASSWORD: {{ db_password }} ESPOCRM_ADMIN_USERNAME: {{ admin_username }} ESPOCRM_ADMIN_PASSWORD: {{ admin_password }} ESPOCRM_SITE_URL: https://{{ domain }} volumes: - ./data:/var/www/html ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network espocrm-daemon: image: espocrm/espocrm:latest container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_daemon' depends_on: - espocrm volumes: - ./data:/var/www/html entrypoint: docker-daemon.sh restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN de EspoCRM. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con # `-p` y password vacío mysql/mariadb espera un prompt por stdin y CUELGA. Prefiere el # usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si no hay usuario de app # (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T db sh -c ' # MySQL 8 trae mysqldump/mysql; MariaDB renombró a mariadb-dump/mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d db # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T db sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
Root password for the MySQL database server.
Password for the EspoCRM application database user.
Password for the initial EspoCRM administrator account.
Username of the initial EspoCRM administrator account.
